items.title: Six children among 10 hurt in three-vehicle crash near Bentong
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 26 — Six children were among 10 people injured in a three-vehicle crash along Jalan Mempaga–Raub near Kem Smart BKERP in Kampung Lebu, Bentong, today. Pahang Fire and Rescue Department (JBPM) assistant director of operations Mohd Salahuddin Isa said two victims suffered relatively serious injuries to their waist and legs, while two other occupants of one of the vehicles escaped unhurt. Mohd Salahuddin said the victims comprised three men, three women and six children, including three girls.

items.title: Husband recounts missed goodbye before wife's fatal crash
items.summary: GERIK: "Every morning before leaving for work, we would shake hands and kiss each other goodbye. She died at the scene together with Inspector Melvindar Singh Gamum Singh, 41, of the Pengkalan Hulu district police headquarters. Five other teachers travelling with Zuraida were injured.

items.title: Anwar: Govt to build more housing for civil servants
items.summary: DENGKIL: The government will build more housing for civil servants on government-owned land at lower rental rates to help ease their cost of living burden, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said. Anwar said the move followed rising rental rates that have affected civil servants, particularly in major cities. "During my recent visits to Penang, Perak, Negri Sembilan, Johor and Kuala Lumpur, I found that civil servants are facing a serious problem — rising rental rates.

items.title: Boy, 12, caught after six-hour expressway drive, claimed to be adult with dwarfism
items.summary: BEIJING: A 12-year-old boy in southern China was detained after allegedly driving his neighbour's car alone on an expressway for more than six hours before claiming he was an adult with dwarfism when stopped by police. The incident occurred on June 15 after toll staff at Gaotian Toll Station in Guangxi Zhuang Autonomous Region became suspicious when the boy said he did not have enough money to pay the toll, according to the South China Morning Post, citing Guilin Traffic Police. Investigators said Luo allegedly took his neighbour's car keys about 4am and entered the G65 Baomao Expressway via Xiangshan Toll Station in Guilin around 4.45am.

items.title: Isa Samad to remain in jail
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: Former Felda chairman Datuk Seri Mohd Isa Abdul Samad will remain in jail after the Federal Court dismissed his bid to review its earlier decision reinstating his conviction in a corruption case involving RM3.09 million linked to the purchase of Merdeka Palace Hotel & Suites in Kuching, Sarawak. A three-member Federal Court review panel chaired by Chief Judge of Sabah and Sarawak Datuk Azizah Nawawi ruled that Isa's application had no merit, finding that his right to be heard and to appeal had already been afforded in the earlier apex court proceedings. On Feb 10, the Federal Court unanimously reinstated Isa's conviction and sentence after allowing the prosecution's appeal against the Court of Appeal's decision to acquit him on March 6, 2024.

items.title: EC: 593 nomination forms sold ahead of Johor polls, but only 133 candidates pay deposits
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, June 26 — A total of 593 nomination forms have been sold for the Johor state election so far, but only 133 prospective candidates have formally confirmed their participation by paying the required election deposit, the Election Commission (EC) said today. EC chairman Datuk Seri Ramlan Harun said the number is expected to increase as prospective candidates still have until nomination proceedings begin tomorrow morning to complete the necessary formalities. “As of this morning, we have sold 593 nomination forms at Returning Officer (RO) offices throughout the state.

items.title: Search continues for fisherman missing after fall overboard near Pulau Kendi
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: A 51-year-old fisherman is missing after he is believed to have fallen overboard from a fishing boat near Pulau Kendi, Batu Maung, prompting a multi-agency search and rescue operation. Southwest district police chief Assistant Commissioner Anuwal Ab Wahab said police received a report on the incident at 8.53am yesterday involving crewman Tang Chin Khoon. He said Tang was one of four crew members aboard a fishing boat engaged in fishing activities near Pulau Kendi.

items.title: Anwar says no directive issued to stop Onn Hafiz entering Felda settlements
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 26 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today denied claims that anyone had been instructed to prevent Johor caretaker Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi from entering Felda settlements, saying the federal government only handles matters under its own jurisdiction. According to Berita Harian , Anwar said there had been no directive to bar Onn Hafiz from entering Felda areas after the Johor leader claimed he had been prevented from attending a land title presentation ceremony for settlers. Anwar was responding to Onn Hafiz’s allegation that he had been prevented from entering a Felda settlement to present land titles to settlers during the Johor State Felda Settlers’ Day 2026 celebration.

items.title: Bird's nest firm Enest unveils IPO prospectus for ACE listing
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Bird's nest processor and trader Enest Group Bhd has launched its prospectus in conjunction with its initial public offering (IPO) and proposed transfer from the Leap Market to the ACE Market of Bursa Malaysia. Enest said proceeds from the public issue will be used to repay bank borrowings (RM5 million), for working capital (RM6.41 million), and to defray listing expenses (RM3.7 million). Tracing its roots to 2015, the company has grown into an established player in the edible bird's nest industry, focusing on processing and sale of raw, clean edible bird's nest, trading activities, and processed bird's nest products.

items.title: Police hunt man who attacked two cops during arrest
items.summary: SELAMA: Police are hunting for a 39-year-old man who allegedly assaulted two policemen trying to arrest him on Tuesday. District police chief Superintendent Ramzi Mahmudin said the suspect, who is believed to be a drug addict, remains at large, and efforts to track him down are ongoing. Following the incident, Mohd Alwi said police had intensified efforts to locate and apprehend the suspect.

items.title: New low-rent housing projects planned for civil servants nationwide, says Anwar
items.summary: DENGKIL, June 26 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said the government will develop housing for civil servants with lower rental rates as an effort to look after the welfare of the group. “We will develop housing for civil servants with cheap rent. Anwar said that although the government had increased the salaries of civil servants, the issue of increasing rental rates is still a problem in several states.

items.title: Nine arrested as GOF uncovers RM3.75m illegal bauxite operation in Felda plantation
items.summary: Southeast Brigade GOF commander SAC Ahmad Radzi Hussain said the 11.30am operation led to the arrest of four local men and five foreign men believed to be involved in illegal bauxite mining activities. “Preliminary investigations found that the five foreign nationals failed to produce valid work permits and identification documents. The operation involved the intelligence branch of the 7th Battalion GOF Kuantan, the Pahang State Enforcement Unit (UPNP), and the Criminal Investigation Division of the Kuantan District Police Headquarters.

items.title: 23 Rolex watches, 15 jewellery items forfeited to govt
items.summary: Deputy public prosecutor Mahadi Abdul Jumaat said Sessions Court judge Rosli Ahmad made the ruling today after no third party came forward to claim the assets. "The court has allowed the prosecution's application for forfeiture. However, no third party came forward to claim the assets," he said.

items.title: Maserati, Alphard, Rolexes and RM6.3m in drugs seized in crackdown on alleged ‘Datuk’ kingpin
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 26 — A man holding the title ‘Datuk’, believed to be the mastermind of a drug syndicate, was among 291 individuals detained in Op Gaurus conducted by the Bukit Aman Narcotics Criminal Investigation Department (JSJN) across Perak, the Klang Valley and Johor last week. Bukit Aman NCIID director Datuk Hussein Omar Khan said the suspect, in his mid-40s, is believed to be the leader of a secret society active in the states. He said that in the operation carried out from June 17 to 21, police seized various types of drugs, including heroin, MDMA, ketamine, ecstasy pills, Erimin 5 pills and marijuana, with an estimated value of RM6.26 million, and arrested nine syndicate members.

items.title: Sony discontinues Japan sales of robot puppy 'aibo'
items.summary: TOKYO: Sony is halting sales of its robotic puppy "aibo" in Japan, the company said, eight years after the latest model of its interactive android pet became an instant hit. When asked about plans for a potential new generation of aibo, Sony told AFP that "the aibo business will continue." Sony declined to give a total sales figure for the aibo model released in 2018.

items.title: Anwar says top approval rating is no excuse for complacency, vows to work harder
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 26 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today said being named Malaysia’s most popular political leader should not make him complacent, insisting that public approval must instead spur him to improve his government’s performance. The poll placed returning Umno leader Khairy Jamaluddin second with a 50 per cent approval rating, followed by former prime minister Tan Sri Muhyiddin Yassin at 36 per cent. PAS president Tan Sri Abdul Hadi Awang and Umno president and Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Ahmad Zahid Hamidi registered approval ratings of 25 per cent and 24 per cent respectively.

items.title: Four denied bail over alleged Jelutong temple attack sparked by false theft claim
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, June 26 — Four men pleaded not guilty in the Sessions Court here today to charges of being members of an unlawful assembly with the common intention of causing grievous hurt to a man last week. The accused, R. Nahveen Raaj, 34; S. Logakumaran, 31; M. Sanjit Kumar, 38; and K. Dineskumar, 30, were jointly charged with four other individuals who remain at large. They were accused of being members of an unlawful assembly in which one or more members allegedly caused grievous hurt to Mohamed Umar Sheriff Mohamed Ameen, 31.

items.title: Samsung readies US$648bil bet, as AI boom reshapes South Korea
items.summary: SEOUL: Samsung Group plans on Monday to pledge investment of 1,000 trillion won (US$648 billion) in South Korea over 10 years, part of an initiative that aims to turn the country's AI boom into a nationwide growth engine, a media report said. Samsung's investment will include spending on AI data centres, batteries and displays as well as a potential 300 trillion won push to build chip factories in the country's southwest, it said without citing sources. Samsung and SK Hynix declined to comment.

items.title: Caltex brings STEM education drive to Perak
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Caltex Malaysia has expanded its science, technology, engineering and mathematics (STEM) focused education initiative, the Fuel Your School (FYS) programme, to Perak. This is part of the initiative's 11th edition, continuing Caltex Malaysia's push to strengthen science learning and food sustainability in underfunded schools. Implemented in collaboration with MyKasih Foundation and HAVVA Agrotech, and endorsed by the Education Ministry, the programme integrates smart aquaponics systems into school learning environments.

items.title: Malaysia retains top spot as Muslim-friendly travel destination for 11th straight year
items.summary: However, unbeknownst to many, Malaysia is actually the world's leading Muslim-friendly travel destination, and has been for the past 11 years. Malaysia boasts a safe environment, robust halal certification, ubiquitous prayer facilities and family-friendly resorts, making it a top choice among Muslim travellers. This year, Malaysia clinched the Top Muslim-Friendly Destination (OIC) award in the Global Muslim Travel Index (GMTI) 2026 report, presented by Mastercard and CrescentRating.

items.title: MetMalaysia: Rain forecast across much of Johor on state election nomination day
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, June 26 — Rain is forecast to affect seven of the 10 districts in the state tomorrow morning, potentially posing a slight “challenge” for prospective candidates and supporters on nomination day for the Johor state election. Johor director of the Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia), Azlai Ta’at, said rain is expected in Johor Bahru, Muar, Batu Pahat, Kota Tinggi, Pontian, Tangkak and Kulai. Barisan Nasional is also contesting 56 seats, fielding 36 UMNO candidates, 16 MCA candidates and four MIC candidates.

items.title: Johor police announce road closures and diversions for state election nomination day
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, June 26 — Several major roads near nomination centres in the state will be closed or diverted in stages to facilitate nomination day for the 16th Johor state election tomorrow. Johor police chief Datuk Ab Rahaman Arsad said the closures and diversions are to ensure safety and smooth conduct of the nomination process. The roads include Jalan Utama Dato’ Onn and Jalan Ayer Molek in Johor Bahru, Jalan Persiaran Indahpura 6 in Kulai, Jalan Omar in Batu Pahat, Jalan Arab and Jalan Petri in Muar, and Jalan Dato’ Teoh Siew Khor and Jalan Sultan in Kluang.

items.title: Over 100 Buddhist police personnel join special Police Day prayers
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: More than 100 Buddhist officers and personnel from Bukit Aman and the Kuala Lumpur contingent attended a special prayer ceremony at Buddhist Maha Vihara in Brickfields today in conjunction with the 219th Police Day celebration. The annual programme, which began at 8am, brought together serving and retired police personnel, religious leaders and invited guests to receive blessings from Buddhist Chief Priest of Malaysia Venerable Datuk Dr K. Sri Dhammaratanna. Inspector-General of Police Tan Sri Mohd Khalid Ismail's speech was delivered by Commercial Crime Investigation Department assistant director (Forgery and Syndicated Investment Scam Investigation) Senior Assistant Commissioner Marvin Tan Seng Kian.

items.title: RM200mil allocated for non-Muslim houses of worship maintenance, says Nga
items.summary: KLUANG: The Housing and Local Government Ministry has allocated RM200 million for the maintenance of non-Muslim houses of worship (RIBI) nationwide over a four-year period beginning 2023. Minister Nga Kor Ming said the initiative covered maintenance works for churches, gurdwaras, Hindu temples and association premises, reflecting what he described as the Madani government's commitment to fairness across all communities. He said the ministry had received 1,478 applications via the e-RIBI system, valued at more than RM279 million, reflecting continued demand for upgrades and maintenance of such premises.

items.title: Anwar urges parties to keep Johor campaign civil, cautions against arrogance ahead of polls
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 26 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today urged parties contesting the Johor state election to campaign with civility and humility, cautioning against arrogance even when promoting their achievements to voters. According to Utusan Malaysia , Anwar said fierce contests, including the prospect of three-cornered fights, were a normal part of democracy, but stressed that campaigning should remain respectful and guided by good values. The prime minister, who is also Pakatan Harapan (PH) chairman, said he had reminded the coalition’s election machinery not to become overconfident despite its track record.

items.title: KLIMS 2026 draws over 205,000 visitors
items.summary: Organised by the Malaysian Automotive Association (MAA) and managed by Qube Integrated Malaysia Sdn Bhd, the show carried the theme "Beyond Mobility", highlighting the automotive industry's shift towards electrification, greener technologies and intelligent mobility solutions. Spanning seven halls, the event featured leading automotive brands including Proton, Perodua, Honda, Hyundai, Toyota, Mazda, Nissan, MG Motor, Xpeng, Zeekr and GWM, among others. MAA president Mohd Shamsor Mohd Zain said KLIMS has evolved beyond a traditional motor show.

items.title: Selangor Sultan's younger sister dies
items.summary: SHAH ALAM: The younger sister of the Sultan of Selangor, Tengku Putri Arafiah Sultan Salahuddin Abdul Aziz Shah, died at her home in Section 12 today. "The remains of Tengku Putri Arafiah will be taken to the Sultan Salahuddin Abdul Aziz Shah Mosque in Shah Alam, where funeral prayers will be held after Friday prayers. The Sultan of Selangor, Sultan Sharafuddin Idris Shah, is expected to attend the funeral prayers at the mosque.

items.title: Court forfeits Rolex watches, jewellery seized from ex-army chief’s sister-in-law to govt
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 26 — A total of 23 Rolex watches and 15 pieces of jewellery seized by the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) from Nor Shahira Atirah Anuar @ Kamaruddin, the sister-in-law of former Army Chief Tan Sri Muhammad Hafizuddeain Jantan, were officially forfeited to the government today. Lawyer Muhammad Kausar Mohd Khairi, representing Nor Shahira Atirah, confirmed the outcome. In earlier proceedings, the MACC had applied for a court order to publish a gazette notice calling on any persons with an interest in the seized items to appear in court, pursuant to Section 41(2) of the MACC Act 2009.

items.title: Johari proposes proportional representation to strengthen minority voice in Parliament
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Dewan Rakyat Speaker Tan Sri Johari Abdul has mooted the introduction of a proportional representation system, saying it could help develop a more inclusive generation of leaders that better reflects Malaysia's ethnic and cultural diversity. He said such a system would prepare the country for shifting demographics while ensuring minority communities continue to have representation in Parliament. Citing projections, Johari said that Bumiputera Malays were expected to make up 77 per cent of the population by 2050, raising questions over future minority representation.

items.title: Kuching court sentences Serian man who raped 15-year-old neighbour to 30 years’ jail, 18 lashes
items.summary: KUCHING, June 26 — The Sessions Court here has sentenced a 28-year-old man to a total of 30 years in prison and 18 strokes of the rotan for statutory rape. Suspecting that her daughter had been raped, the mother lodged a police report, prompting police action and a medical examination of the victim at hospital. Acting on information received, the police subsequently detained the accused on June 18.

items.title: 19 roads to be closed or diverted for Johor nomination day tomorrow
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Several major roads near nomination centres across Johor will be closed or diverted in stages tomorrow in conjunction with nomination day for the 16th Johor election. Johor police chief Datuk Ab Rahaman Arsad said the measures were to ensure safety and smooth traffic flow during the nomination process. Among them are Jalan Utama Dato' Onn and Jalan Ayer Molek in Johor Bahru, Jalan Persiaran Indahpura 6 in Kulai, Jalan Omar in Batu Pahat, Jalan Arab and Jalan Petri in Muar, as well as Jalan Dato' Teoh Siew Khor and Jalan Sultan in Kluang.

items.title: Cockroaches, rats: DBKL issues 74 notices, shuts six Jalan Alor eateries
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Kuala Lumpur City Hall (DBKL) issued 74 notices and compounds against food premises during a special enforcement operation around Jalan Alor, Bukit Bintang, yesterday. The notices and compounds were issued under several laws, including the Federal Territory of Kuala Lumpur Food Establishment Licensing By-Laws 2016, the Federal Territory Food Handlers By-Laws 1979, the Street, Drainage and Building Act 1974, the Federal Territory of Kuala Lumpur Trade, Business and Industry Licensing By-Laws 2016, and the Control of Smoking Products for Public Health Act 2024. Meanwhile, six food premises were ordered to close under By-Law 28(1) of the Federal Territory of Kuala Lumpur Food Establishment Licensing By-Laws 2016 for failing to comply with prescribed hygiene requirements.

items.title: Police seek saliva test kits for roadblocks to detect ‘Piu Piu’, ‘Magic Mushroom’ vape users
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 26 — Police are seeking to deploy saliva testing kits at roadblocks by the end of the year to detect motorists under the influence of synthetic drugs marketed as “Piu Piu” and “Magic Mushroom”, amid concerns the substances are contributing to road accidents. According to Berita Harian , Bukit Aman Narcotics Criminal Investigation Department director Datuk Hussein Omar Khan said the move comes as police have detected more cases involving motorists believed to have been impaired after inhaling synthetic drug liquids through electronic cigarettes or vape devices. Hussein explained that “Piu Piu” and “Magic Mushroom” are not the names of specific drugs but commercial terms used to market synthetic drug liquids mixed into vape devices.

items.title: Syed Saddiq leaves fate to God as Federal Court ruling nears
items.summary: PETALING JAYA: Muar MP Syed Saddiq Syed Abdul Rahman says he is leaving his fate to God as he awaits the Federal Court's decision on the prosecution's final appeal in his case, due in four days. The former youth and sports minister said he remained calm ahead of the ruling and confident in the judicial process he had gone through for seven years. The former Muda president, is facing charges of criminal breach of trust, dishonest misappropriation of property and money laundering involving Bersatu Youth (Armada) funds.

items.title: RTM, Astro strengthen partnership to expand broadcast reach
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Radio Television Malaysia (RTM) and Astro have agreed to strengthen strategic cooperation to widen access to quality content across multiple broadcasting platforms. In a joint statement, both parties said the expanded collaboration would see RTM channels made more widely available on Astro, including Sukan+, Berita RTM and RTM's radio network. The expanded lineup, including Berita RTM, Sukan+ and RTM radio stations, will be made available to Astro customers in the near future.

items.title: Study finds Malaysian women often kept quiet about sexual violence over fears they would not be believed
items.summary: This means the study was on both Muslim and non-Muslim women, with the respondents also of various ethnicities: Malay (62 per cent); Chinese (21 per cent); other Bumiputera (10 per cent) and Indian (four per cent). Out of the 1,004 women aged 18 to 65 surveyed, 43 per cent said they had experienced sexual violence, with the most common types including groping, unwanted sexual comments and online sexual harassment. The most common places where these women experienced sexual violence were online (40 per cent), in public spaces (38 per cent), workplaces (21 per cent), homes (11 per cent), and educational institutions (7 per cent).

items.title: APHM working with govt to tackle rising medical costs
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Association of Private Hospitals Malaysia (APHM) says it is working closely with the government to address rising medical costs through its role in the Joint Ministerial Committee on Private Healthcare Costs (JMCPHC). Its president, Datuk Dr Kuljit Singh, said the association supported initiatives by the Health Ministry and Finance Ministry to tackle medical cost inflation, including issues raised in the Public Accounts Committee (PAC) report, many of which are already being addressed through the committee. "APHM continues to support broader efforts of the JMCPHC to manage rising healthcare costs by contributing industry data and providing constructive input to inform policy development," he said in a statement.

items.title: Mahfuz: Pas must explain backing for Bersatu despite allegations
items.summary: Amanah vice-president Mahfuz Omar said Pas leaders had previously accused Bersatu of betrayal in Perlis, undermining political efforts in Negri Sembilan, violating syariah principles in political cooperation, weakening unity within PN, being greedy in seat negotiations, lacking grassroots machinery and taking advantage of Pas. If these allegations are true, why is Pas still allowing Bersatu to contest 16 seats in the Johor election under the PN banner? he said.

items.title: Musk's SpaceX targets US consumers with Starlink mobile service push
items.summary: CALIFORNIA: SpaceX has told investors it plans to launch a Starlink mobile service for US consumers, the Financial Times reported on Friday, in a move that could allow the Elon Musk-led company to compete directly with Verizon , AT&T and T-Mobile. SpaceX already offers direct-to-cell connectivity with T-Mobile in the US, providing supplemental coverage from space to extend internet access to remote areas. SpaceX is now considering launching a Starlink retail product and could build its own terrestrial US mobile network, President Gwynne Shotwell told investors during a recent IPO roadshow, the FT report said, citing sources.

items.title: Isa Samad conviction review: Federal Court to decide fate after Friday prayers
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: Datuk Mohd Isa Abdul Samad will know after Friday prayers today whether the Federal Court will allow his bid to review its decision reinstating his six-year jail sentence and RM15.45 million fine for corruption. The former Felda chairman is seeking leave under Rule 137 of the Rules of the Federal Court 1995 to review the apex court's Feb 10 decision, claiming there was a breach of natural justice. Mohd Isa, 77, has been serving his six-year prison sentence at Sungai Buloh Prison since Feb 10 after the Federal Court allowed the prosecution's appeal and reinstated the High Court's conviction, sentence and fine.

items.title: Palm oil rises tracking Dalian, but on track for a weekly loss
items.summary: JAKARTA: Malaysian palm oil futures rose on Friday, tracking strength in rival vegetable oils in the Dalian market, although futures were on track for a weekly loss. Dalian's most-active soyoil contract rose 0.87 per cent while its palm oil contract gained 1.3 per cent. According to independent inspection company AmSpec Agri Malaysia, exports of Malaysian palm oil products for June 1 to 25 rose 11.1 per cent, while according to cargo surveyor Intertek Testing Services it rose 10.6 per cent.

items.title: Kioxia shares slump 12pct as AI-related stocks fall
items.summary: TOKYO: Shares of Japanese chipmaker Kioxia slid 12 per cent on Friday after a report that ChatGPT maker OpenAI was considering delaying its initial public offering sparked a selloff in AI-related shares. Kioxia, previously called Toshiba Memory and carved out of Toshiba in 2018, is a major producer of memory chips. Kioxia said on Thursday it is considering a stock split and aims to list American depositary shares on a US exchange at the beginning of the next financial year, which runs until March 2028.

items.title: Selangor targets bigger share of Malaysia’s aerospace industry by 2030, says state exco
items.summary: SERDANG, June 26 — Selangor is aiming to further strengthen its position as Malaysia’s leading aerospace hub and a key regional player by expanding its maintenance, repair and overhaul (MRO) capabilities, developing skilled talent and enhancing its investment ecosystem. Ng said Selangor also aims to produce about 22,000 skilled aerospace workers by 2030 through investments in education and training to meet growing industry demand. The Selangor Aerospace Forum convenes today in conjunction with the MyAERO Summit 2026, Malaysia’s premier aerospace and future mobility event.

items.title: Johor polls: BN, PH, PN set for clashes in 33 of 56 state seats
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Barisan Nasional (BN), Pakatan Harapan (PH) and Perikatan Nasional (PN) are expected to clash in 33 of the 56 state seats in the Johor election next month. BN and PH are contesting all 56 seats, while PN has distributed its 33 seats among Bersatu, Pas, Pejuang and the Malaysian Indian People's Party (MIPP). The 16 seats seeing direct three-cornered fights between BN, PH and Bersatu are Kemelah, Bukit Kepong, Bukit Pasir, Serom, Bukit Naning, Semarang, Parit Raja, Senggarang, Rengit, Layang-Layang, Tenggaroh, Panti, Sedili, Johor Lama, Penawar and Stulang.

items.title: Psychiatrist tells inquest Zara Qairina endured mounting peer pressure before death
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, June 26 — The Coroner’s Court heard today that Zara Qairina Mahathir had endured repeated emotional pressure linked to peer relationships in the months leading up to her death. Dr Wong revealed that between March and May 2025, there were mounting signs that Zara Qairina was struggling with tension among peers, emotional insecurity, fear of confrontation, anxiety over senior students and reports of mocking or humiliating treatment. “By June and July 2025, the materials reflected progressive emotional distress, heightened rejection sensitivity, increased interpersonal vigilance and diminished perceived emotional safety,” he said, reading his witness statement during the inquest proceedings before Coroner Amir Shah Amir Hassan.

items.title: Pas says it will not help Bersatu campaign in Johor polls despite PN banner
items.summary: MARANG: Pas will not deploy its election machinery to help Bersatu campaign in the 16th Johor state election, despite both parties contesting under the Perikatan Nasional (PN) banner. Pas president Tan Sri Abdul Hadi Awang said the two parties would use separate machinery and campaign strategies, although they would share the PN logo in the July 11 polls. We remain with PN.

items.title: Diesel-powered MPV owners should get extra 100 litres subsidy, says MAA
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 26 — The Malaysian Automotive Association (MAA) hopes the government will consider extending the application for the additional 100 litres of subsidised diesel to multi-purpose vehicle (MPV) owners. In a statement, the association said that currently, the measure is only available to eligible pick-up trucks and sport utility vehicle (SUV) owners. On June 22, the government decided to provide an additional 100 litres of subsidised diesel on top of the existing 200 litres allocation for eligible owners of pick-up trucks and SUVs.

items.title: Labour policies must address underemployment, skills mismatch — MEF
items.summary: Its president Datuk Dr Syed Hussain Syed Husman said while Malaysia has one of the region's most comprehensive support systems for displaced workers, the next priority should be improving workforce adaptability. Syed Hussain called for stronger collaboration between the government, employers and workers to build a more resilient labour market that supports sustainable, meaningful employment. He also welcomed the government's continued commitment to helping displaced workers return to work through labour market institutions, social protection mechanisms, and reskilling and upskilling programmes.

items.title: Homeless man detained after allegedly hurling bricks at police vehicle outside Jalan Hang Lekiu hotel
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 26 — Police have arrested a homeless Myanmar national after he damaged a police vehicle by throwing bricks in front of a hotel on Jalan Hang Lekiu here, on Wednesday. Dang Wangi district deputy police chief Supt Nuzulan Mohd Din in a statement today said the man in his 40s, was later arrested with the help of the public at 11.57 pm. Nuzulan said police were continuing their investigation under Section 427 of the Penal Code and Section 6(1)© of the Immigration Act 1959/63.

items.title: No help for Bersatu from PAS election machinery in Johor, Hadi says
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 26 — PAS will not deploy its election machinery to help Parti Pribumi Bersatu Malaysia candidates in next month’s Johor state election despite both parties contesting under the Perikatan Nasional (PN) banner, party president Tan Sri Abdul Hadi Awang said today. According to Berita Harian , Abdul Hadi said PAS had ended its political cooperation with Bersatu and that the two parties would run separate campaign operations, even though Bersatu had insisted on using the PN logo. “PAS has already terminated its political cooperation with Bersatu.

items.title: PAS sets sights on Opposition role in Johor instead of forming state government, says Hadi
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 26 — PAS has set its sights on becoming Johor’s Opposition rather than forming the next state government, with party president Tan Sri Abdul Hadi Awang saying its goal in the state election is to serve as a check on the administration rather than take power. According to Utusan Malaysia , Abdul Hadi said PAS was aiming to become “the Opposition and an adviser to the state government”, rather than form the next administration. “We are contesting 11 state seats.

items.title: Psychiatrist tells inquest Zara Qairina was emotionally overwhelmed after school confrontation
items.summary: Zara reportedly denied wrongdoing and became visibly distressed,” he said. Under examination by counsel Shahlan Jufri, the witness testified that the audio recordings between Zara Qairina and her mother were also clinically important. “From a psychiatric perspective, Zara’s mother appeared to function as a primary source of emotional containment, reassurance, interpersonal processing, and psychological safety for Zara,” he explained.

items.title: Jealousy over chat with male neighbour leaves Sabah mum of six fighting for life after husband allegedly slashes her 14 times
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 26 — A mother of six is fighting for her life after her husband allegedly slashed her 14 times with a parang in an attack police believe was fuelled by jealousy over her speaking to a male neighbour in Sabah’s Kota Marudu district. Kota Marudu police chief Superintendent Somiun Lomidin said preliminary investigations indicated the suspect believed his wife was having an affair with a male neighbour, allegations she had repeatedly denied. According to police, the suspect became upset after seeing the victim chatting with the neighbour from the compound of their home.

items.title: Woman accused of starving 41 cats in Subang Jaya apartment claims trial, granted RM10,000 bail
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 26 — A freelance worker pleaded not guilty in the Petaling Jaya Sessions Court today to two animal cruelty charges after prosecutors alleged she neglected 41 cats at an apartment in Subang Jaya, causing several of the animals to die from starvation. Lee Reo Jin entered her plea before Sessions Court Judge Noor Ruzilawati Mohd Noor, Free Malaysia Today reported. Prosecutors alleged that Lee failed to provide adequate care for the cats, leaving them without sufficient food until they starved.

items.title: Hadi says PAS-Bersatu split is real, not an election tactic despite shared Perikatan banner in Johor
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 26 — PAS president Tan Sri Abdul Hadi Awang today insisted his party’s split with Parti Pribumi Bersatu Malaysia is genuine and not an election tactic. He also said political cooperation between the two parties has already ended despite both continuing to contest under the Perikatan Nasional (PN) banner for the Johor state election. Speaking after delivering a morning religious lecture at Rusila Mosque in Marang today, Abdul Hadi said PAS and Bersatu were campaigning separately because Bersatu had insisted on using the PN logo.

items.title: Report: Malaysia tourism to rebound H2 2026 from Middle East conflict disruption, arrivals set to top pre-pandemic levels
items.summary: In a report released yesterday, BMI forecast Malaysia would receive 27.97 million international tourist arrivals this year, representing a 5.1 per cent increase from 2025 and 7.2 per cent above the 2019 pre-pandemic benchmark. Year-to-date arrivals reached 10.6 million, up 1.1 per cent from the same period last year. “Based on the first five months of arrivals data, we believe the slowdown is cyclical rather than structural,” BMI said.

items.title: Fahmi extends condolences to family of former MyGCC chief Yazman Yahya
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 26 — Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil has expressed his condolences to the family of former Malaysia Government Call Centre (MyGCC) branch head Yazman Yahya who passed away this morning. “Inna lillahi wa inna ilaihi raji’un. MyGCC functions under the Ministry of Communications as a local call centre and the official communications hub of the Malaysian government.

items.title: Six Jalan Alor eateries shut after DBKL raid uncovers rats, cockroaches and filthy kitchens
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 26 — Six eateries along Kuala Lumpur’s famed Jalan Alor food street have been ordered to close after a surprise late-night enforcement operation uncovered serious hygiene violations, including rat and cockroach infestations, improperly stored food and food handlers who had not received mandatory typhoid vaccinations. In a statement today, DBKL said the operation was conducted “to ensure the standards of cleanliness, food safety and compliance with licensing conditions at food premises are always at the prescribed level.” The city authority issued 74 notices and compounds under various laws, including the Kuala Lumpur Food Establishment Licensing By-Laws 2016, the Federal Territory Food Handlers By-Laws 1979, the Street, Drainage and Building Act 1974, the Kuala Lumpur Trade, Business and Industry Licensing By-Laws 2016, and the Control of Smoking Products for Public Health Act 2024. As a result, six food premises were ordered to close under By-Law 28(1) of the Kuala Lumpur Food Establishment Licensing By-Laws 2016 “for failing to comply with the prescribed hygiene requirements,” DBKL said.

items.title: Bodies of two fishermen found after boat capsizes off Kuala Trong
items.summary: IPOH, June 26 — The bodies of two fishermen whose boat capsized off Pulau Pasir Hitam, Kuala Trong, near Bukit Gantang, on Wednesday were found yesterday. Malaysian Maritime Enforcement Agency (MMEA) zone director Commander Mohd Hairil Othman said the bodies were found by a local fishing vessel at about 5 pm. Mohd Hairil reminded fishermen to always prioritise safety while at sea by ensuring their boats are in good and seaworthy condition, wearing life jackets at all times, carrying functioning communication equipment, and checking the weather forecast before heading out to sea.

items.title: Johor polls enter full swing tomorrow as political heavyweights prepare for nomination day fight
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, June 26 — The 16th Johor state election swings into full gear tomorrow as nomination day sets the stage for a high-stakes battle across all 56 state assembly seats. Polling day is set for July 11, with early voting on July 7. With all eyes on nomination day, the EC is urging prospective candidates to double-check their nomination forms early at the Returning Officer’s Office or State Election Office to avoid any last-minute hiccups.
